steps.title: Set up the percent increase formula
steps.content:
We know there were 99 dragonflies on February 15, with a 12.50% increase from January 1. Using the percent increase formula:
$$\text{Percent Increase} = \frac{\text{Final} - \text{Initial}}{\text{Initial}} \times 100$$
$$12.50 = \frac{99 - x}{x} \times 100$$
where $x$ is the initial number on January 1.

steps.title: Backsolve by testing the choices
steps.content:
Backsolving — testing choice B (87):
$$\frac{99 - 87}{87} \times 100 = \frac{12}{87} \times 100 \approx 13.79\%$$
Fails — we need 12.50%, not 13.79%. Testing choice A (88):
$$\frac{99 - 88}{88} \times 100 = \frac{11}{88} \times 100 = \frac{1}{8} \times 100 = 12.5\%$$
That confirms A is correct.
